name: Berrocal - El Madroño – circular by the Río Tinto and Arroyo del Gallego
name_original: Berrocal - El Madroño – circular por el Río Tinto y Arroyo del Gallego
description: This difficult 14.5-mile circular hike near Berrocal explores the unique red waters of the Río Tinto and historical mining landscapes.
region_intro: Hiking around Berrocal offers routes through a landscape shaped by its mining heritage and distinctive natural features. The region is characterized by mountainous terrain, red-tinged hills, and impressive rock formations. A prominent feature is the Río Tinto, known for its vivid reddish-orange waters due to high iron content. Hikers can explore scenic forests and areas with unique microclimates. Best hiking trails around Berrocal The most popular hiking route is Antiguas Minas de Riotinto - circular route, a 4.8 miles (7.7 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 6 minutes to complete, exploring the area's mining history. Another top favourite among local hikers is Río Tinto Greenway Loop, a moderate 5.1 miles (8.2 km) path. This route follows the unique reddish landscape influenced by the Río Tinto. Local hikers also love the On the banks of the Riotinto along the old railway, a 5.6 miles (9.0 km) trail leading through the distinctive river banks and historical railway, often completed in about 2 hours 36 minutes. Hiking around Berrocal is defined by the unique Río Tinto, red-tinged hills, and remnants of its mining heritage. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult trails. The routes in Berrocal are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Berrocal's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Berrocal?
A: Berrocal offers a selection of over 15 hiking trails, catering to various fitness levels. These routes explore the region's unique landscapes, including its mining heritage and the distinctive Río Tinto.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of hiking trails in Berrocal?
A: The trails around Berrocal range from easy to difficult. There are 2 easy routes, 11 moderate routes, and 3 difficult routes, ensuring options for hikers of all experience levels.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Berrocal?
A: Yes, Berrocal features several circular routes. A notable one is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983767536/antiguas-minas-de-riotinto-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Antiguas Minas de Riotinto - circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 4.8-mile trail exploring the area's mining history. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984221964/berrocal-el-madrono-circular-by-the-rio-tinto-and-arroyo-del-gallego" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Berrocal - El Madroño – circular by the Río Tinto and Arroyo del Gallego&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 14.5-mile trek.

Q: What unique natural features can I expect to see while hiking in Berrocal?
A: Hikers in Berrocal will encounter the striking Río Tinto, famous for its vivid reddish-orange waters due to high iron content. The region also boasts mountainous terrain, distinctive red-tinged hills, and impressive rock formations like Los Risco Altos, offering a truly unique landscape.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions along the hiking trails?
A: Yes, Berrocal's mining heritage is a significant part of its landscape. You can explore sites like the historically significant &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5287308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corta Atalaya Mine&lt;/a&gt;, a massive open-pit mine. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5345695" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cachán Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, an old railway bridge, also provides access to paths along the red river, blending history with nature.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Berrocal?
A: The hiking routes in Berrocal are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 30 reviews. Hikers often praise the unique reddish landscape shaped by the Río Tinto and the rich mining history that can be explored along the paths.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking options in Berrocal?
A: For those seeking a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010392406/el-madrono-miners-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Madroño Miners' Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 11.6-mile route. This trail offers a more extensive exploration of the region's terrain and mining heritage.

Q: Can I hike along the Río Tinto?
A: Absolutely. The Río Tinto is a central feature of many hikes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1144750763/rio-tinto-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Tinto Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.1-mile path that follows the unique reddish landscape influenced by the river. Another option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1144803253/on-the-banks-of-the-riotinto-along-the-old-railway" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;On the banks of the Riotinto along the old railway&lt;/a&gt;, a 5.6-mile trail leading through the distinctive river banks and historical railway.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect beyond the red river?
A: Beyond the striking red river and mining landscapes, Berrocal also features scenic forests and areas with unique microclimates, such as La Fuente. These offer diverse flora and fauna, providing a different kind of natural beauty and shade for hikers.

Q: Is Berrocal suitable for nature enthusiasts looking for unique ecosystems?
A: Yes, Berrocal is an excellent destination for nature enthusiasts. The region's unique geology, particularly the Río Tinto, creates an unusual ecosystem. Additionally, areas like La Fuente are ecologically valuable, boasting a unique microclimate that supports diverse flora and fauna.
tips: Beautiful views of the Rio Tinto valley from this panoramic point. The river provides that ocher color and together with the environment that surrounds it, they make up an almost Martian landscape, from another planet.

This small but picturesque town in the interior of Seville borders the province of Huelva to the west and you can get here following some paths next to the Mining Basin. Among its places of interest, the Church of San Blas and the beautiful Hermitage next to the Town Hall stand out.
From the bridge over the Gallego stream there is a slight ascent, smooth except for a curved section and with a slope of almost 8%. The landscape has recovered well after the 2004 fire.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Berrocal - El Madroño circular hike?
faqs.answer: This trail is classified as difficult. It requires very good physical fitness, sure-footedness, and appropriate footwear. It's best suited for experienced hikers due to its length (23.4 km) and elevation gain (590 meters).

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular hike?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for completing this hike is approximately 6 hours and 45 minutes, assuming a steady pace.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied. You'll experience a beautiful downhill path from El Madroño through green fields with cork oaks, followed by the largely flat "Sendero de los Mineros" along the Jarrama reservoir. Be prepared for some steep or uneven sections given the overall difficulty rating.

faqs.question: What are the main natural and historical highlights along the trail?
faqs.answer: The most striking feature is the Río Tinto Viewpoint, where you'll see the river's unique reddish waters. You'll also pass by the serene Jarrama reservoir and the "Sendero de los Mineros," offering views of ancient mining extractions. The route also traverses the provincial border between Seville and Huelva.

faqs.question: Is the Berrocal - El Madroño circular trail suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: No, due to its 'difficult' classification, length, and elevation gain, this trail is generally not recommended for beginners or families with young children. It requires a good level of physical fitness and hiking experience.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail can be enjoyed year-round, spring and autumn typically offer the most pleasant temperatures for a demanding hike like this. Summers can be very hot in Andalusia, and winters might bring cooler, potentially wetter conditions.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Berrocal - El Madroño circular trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally dog-friendly, but it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas, near livestock, or in sections where wildlife might be present. Ensure your dog is also fit enough for a long, difficult hike.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike this route?
faqs.answer: There is no specific permit or entrance fee required for individual hikers to access this trail. However, always respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details are not provided, trailheads for circular routes like this often have designated parking areas in or near the starting village, such as Berrocal or El Madroño. It's recommended to check local maps or komoot's detailed tour information for precise starting points and parking options.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of the Vía Verde del Río Tinto, the Camino de las Eras, Aldea Las Delgadas a Via Verde del Río Tinto, Puente Cachá, and Carril Aldea Las Delgadas a Via Verde del Río Tinto.

faqs.question: What makes the Río Tinto so unique?
faqs.answer: The Río Tinto is famous for its intense red and orange hues, caused by high iron and mineral content. Its waters are notably devoid of oxygen and fish but host unique microorganisms that thrive in this extreme environment, creating a truly otherworldly landscape.
